name: German Pancakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together flour and salt. Add alternately with milk to the beaten eggs a little at a time while beating briskly.
2. Spread butter evenly on the bottom and sides of a well-seasoned 10-inch cast iron skillet.
3. Pour in the batter and place the pan in the pre-heated oven set to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
4. Bake for 20-25 minutes, gradually reducing the heat to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
5. The pancake is done when it puffs up high above the sides of the pan and turns golden brown. Slide the pancake onto a plate, drizzle with butter, and sprinkle generously with powdered sugar.
6. Drizzle lemon juice over the sugar. Serve immediately.
This recipe will also cook nicely in 2 8-inch pans for individual-sized pancakes.
